title_guess: Spain & France 2008: Bilbao ferry, Burgos, Zamora, Salamanca (the long summer tour)

pages.text:
__Sunday July 27th__
	Simon, Charles & Eve set off for Spain & France. We went down the M5 & changed to the M4 near Swindon. Simon said "Conta lives over there," & I had just written her a letter. So when we passed "Hunton Parva" I said "Let's go & see her new house. After much searching, we found it. Lots of potential. In Savernake Forest we had lunch in the shade. Then we went on to Venetia's for 3.30.
	Olivia & Simon were there as well as Venetia & David. Soon Thomas & Naomi & Austin arrived. They were late because their car had overheated. We had a lovely tea & chatted.
	At 6 we left for the ferry. We seemed to spend hours on the quai.
	We ate supper as we waited for the ship to leave port, on deck, with an [illegible] annoying smoker next to us, who

chain-smoked throughout. Naomi worked out she must spend £4,000 a year on ciggies.

   In fact it was always difficult to find a space on deck where there wasn't cigarette smoke. And the people were fat fat FAT. Even the cabaret singer was FAT. We had just seen Wall-e so we ought to know.

   The next day we took it in turns to look after Austin, Simon, Charles & I, so that Thomas & Naomi could go to a movie. We saw dolphins, but poor Naomi didn't see them, or a whale.

   __Tuesday__
   At a truly horrendous early hour (6) we had breakfast & left the ferry at 7. We meandered along looking at the plains at Amurria we stopped & walked around. We shopped at a supermarket & had lunch in Burgos in the country side in shade outside someone's deserted house.

   On to a campsite across the river from Burgos. Simon sent a text to Thomas to see

how they were - they had got no further than a garage near the ferry because they had a leak! Fortunately, so far, the garage has welded a pipe with a hole in it near the radiator, & they may be on the road again when it cools.

   We've been swimming.

   I forgot to mention that after leaving Venetia Thomas put a lead-free petrol nozzle in his tank & was only reminded at the last minute by Naomi that he ought to be using diesel. Happy Holidays!

   __Wednesday July 30th__
   We drove off to Burgos & parked near the river. There was [illegible sketch/drawing] a beautiful metal sculpture.

   In a big square we had lunch - pizza for €6, chicken & chips & salad €6.50 & veal & chips. While I was watching 2 small children ran about on a metal stage, watched by their

__BURGOS__

A very fine cathedral, carvings everywhere. It's on the [crossed out] pilgrimage route. The museum & treasure room is well-laid out: Ivory & alabastre carvings: Flemish paintings. Silver work. Lovely wood carvings in the

father. It was as high as my neck. The father turned away just as the boy launched himself into his father's arms, so the child fell onto the cobble stones. He cried as he went out of the Square with his parents.

choir. Doors - one of the Entry into ~~Jera~~ Jerusalem across from the Harrowing of Hell, in wood.

	One of the most interesting Cathedrals I've seen.

	Lots of ducks on the river. Lots of sparrows in the campsite.

	We drove on & had tea by a station below a "finca" or country estate. The deserted station seemed rather like America

	On ~~the~~ to __Zamora__, where Wellington had a big battle. ~~The campsite woman~~ crossed the river, deep ravines, to get onto the north bank of the [?Anero]. The French didn't think it could be done (everyone else had come up from the south.) Wellington himself was pulled across in a hammock on ropes.

captions: CATEDRAL DE BURGOS — PATRIMONIO DE LA HUMANIDAD
pages.text:
Gold Staircase 1519-1526 Diego de [?Siloe]

The cathedral is up against a hill. This staircase leads up to a door at street level.
After parking the van, Simon & Eve swam in a lovely pool with a very fat lady in a blue bikini.

captions: Saint Anne Room, Beaune, former linen room, P 20,
Tapastry, miracle of Saint Eloi. Beaune.
pages.text:
Saint Anne Room, Beaune, former linen room, P 20,

Tapastry, miracle of Saint Eloi. Beaune.

Youth hostel & camping, Lorde valley.
04.71.23.56.
10/06.07.88.
19.04.
15110 [?Fridefont]
Auvergne
Ferme Auberge.

Friday 15  we left the village, I stopped to look in the Chapel. It was rather Sad. It advertised itself as a museum for the art work of village churches, that had been submerged by the dam & reservoir. But a sign in the side chapel said that the statues — several old wooden statues, including 2 of St. Roch, had been stolen by thieves one night in 2001. There were only photos.

   We parked & walked. It was a fabulous walk — out to some erratic boulders, down along the cliff path of the Gorges de Ses. It was uphill, downhill, past a water house. This took water from way back along the cliff face, a canal or pipes, with intermittent servicing caves which we passed. The water was collected in 2 huge red pipes & shot down the steep sides of the gorge.
   The path at times felt very dangerous. It went past rocky outcrops called "the Eagles' beak", or Devil's something else. I started feeling sick where I had to sit on my bum to slither down the narrow path & dislodged stones slipped off down into the torrent way below.
   We came up onto the plateau & walked back past farm houses & old roads & paths. A particular feature are the small bread houses — rooms with oven paddles stored up in the rafters & an [illegible]

at one end where bread  is made during the August bread festival.
(oven)
   The old stone [crossed-out: wooden] houses have tremendous stone lintels. Lots of cows, flies, black & blueish beetles, green stripy beetles, frogs, grasshoppers, a lizard. Very warm at times but no Med. plants (olives, palins, figs) oaks, pines, silver birch. When [?Boyer] prospected for the Viaduct it was — 15° & 80° snow.

   After lunch we went to Mallet beach to swim, created at the reservoir where the village was submerged. We hired a canoe — as Simon said — legs in morning, arms in the afternoon. Charles & Eve swam. There was a lifeguard & it was quite warm. There was a green flag — they are careful of raging torrents from the 'electricity co.
   The campsite has a glorious view, but the stupid farmer has plonked an enormous barn on that end of the farm, so humans can't enjoy it.
   Charles' tent was very well populated — [?a earwig] & an overactive grasshopper & noisy frog

captions: VISITA A LA S.I. CATEDRAL DE ZAMORA Y MUSEO
pages.text:
Thursday July 31st 2008            Joan swimming pool
                                   Charles says ok **
We slept in __Zamora campsite__ + drove into
Zamora the next day, parking across the
river. The Cathedral has an amazing, fragile-
looking dome. Simon, Eve & Charles (19) are in
Spain. We arrived here by ferry with Thomas, Nadin
+ Austin who have gone over the Pyrenees in their
green camping van. We are looking at
Wellington's Battle Fields before joining them at
David's house.
   The tapestries in the cathedral museum
were fresh-looking with bright colours. The
misericords had been nailed down by a
bashful bishop because they showed lewd
monks. The present bishop made sure we
didn't get to see them, either, by roping
them off.

We had lunch in town, pork chops, & then drove about 17 miles to S. Pedro, a C- & church, Quite like St. Peter's in Cambridge. It has the crispest Romanesque capitols that I've ever seen - Daniel in the Den + Isaac sacrificing Esau. It had been moved up, stone by stone, to a higher village in 1930's when they flooded the valleys. Also flooded were the ravines which Wellington crossed

when he evaded the French Blockade by going through rough country NW of Zamora. He was pulled across the ravines in a hammock.
	We returned to Zamora & had supper on benches on a little platform overlooking the river. An amazing number of Storks flew past & there were 25 on the finials of the cathedral & the tower.
	At 8.30 we joined a group to look at the Co olive mills, 3 of them, by the river. They've restored the mill wheel & hammer very well.
__Friday August 1st!__
	We set off for Salamanca & parked across the river. We saw the Patio de Escuelas, with the amazing plateresque carved front to the university building, with the symbol for lust - a frog on a skull (see over). We saw the house of shells, & inside it to a lovely courtyard. The old & new cathedrals are beautiful - domes & 1492 [illegible] [illegible]

[Photo/postcard of Salamanca — caption written vertically: Plateresque at Salamanca University]

Charles is amused that ~~Simon~~ Eve is struggling in Italian while Simon pulls out the French.
	We had tapas for lunch which were delicious. Deep-fried shrimp + a fancy thing with beers. Charles, who doesn't like fish

had a pizza.
	We have arrived at an extraordinary campsite __Camping Torres__ by a lake near Salamanca. Everything that can be wrong is wrong + unwelcoming. It reminds Simon of Fawlty Towers. The owners told us to return at 7pm + go to reception to pay. They weren't there. The older man kept pacing up + down watching his 3 year old son? grandson? practising for the Tour de France + finally, when we had sat down for supper, came up + demanded to see our passports. Simon said he'd come after supper. Then the man told Charles to move his washing line, because people might be put off coming because of it. There are only 2 other families here. They have been watering the grass constantly since we arrived. We only saw the sign because we pulled off the road. __CAMP NO[illegible]__
It says:
so no wonder there are so few people.
The washing machine is still on its

packaging (although it's shown in the book as providing a machine.) The taps don't work for providing water for the van. The shower tap took ages to run warm (I counted to 70, a conventional tap) & the foot tray blocked. They were most un welcoming when we arrived.
__Saturday__
   The sprinklers were still on when we left in the morning. We drove to Arapile & Simon & Eve walked up a ridge to where Wellington stood, watching & where he had his glass. There was a gap in the French column, so he sprang an attack & decimated 40,000 French soldiers in 40 minutes. It was the beginning of the end of Napoleon's Europe. They were fighting in 1812, July 21st, only a fortnight before us. The hills are baking hot, the nights cold. No tents were issued until 1813. At the end of the battle, the stubble caught fire & burned up the wounded. Wellington had 52,000 men. The French numbered 50,000. Wellington found a good place to watch from. (He loved riding, whereas his Spanish counterpart, having once

been run over by his cavalry, travelled in a cart drawn by 8 mules.) Wellington hid his men behind the ridge.
——— line (used by English)
————→ column (used by French)
[sketch of concentric squares] Square
Spanish Guerrilla

[Printed brochure page glued in — no handwritten diary text. Printed headings and body text from a museum guide: FRENCH DOLLS; ART DECO GLASS (mentions René Lalique, Ernest Sabino); NANCY SCHOOL GLASS (mentions Emile Gallé, Daum brothers, Majorelle, Legra, Paul Nicolas); PERFUME BOTTLES (mentions René Lalique, Coty, Houbigant, Worth, Guerlain, Gallé, Daum brothers, Baccarat, Saint Louis). Photos of a bisque-headed doll, Art Deco glass vase, amber lamp, and green perfume bottle.]

places: Museum of Art Nouveau + Art Deco
pages.text:
We drove to the end of the Roman Bridge at Salamanca again + walked across + up into the Museum of Art Nouveau + Art Deco in the Casa de Lis. The perfume bottles are lovely the dolls. We spent quite a time discussing them.
We had lunch on the pavement + drove to a le Clerk super market.

On we went to Avila. The heat caught up with us, so we didn't climb the walls. We saw the cathedral. It has a beautiful alabaster altar, but it is roped off so you can't see it. There were more nice alabaster carvings in the musean which we could see.

We drove on to a campsite which is as well run as the last one was badly run. The man at the accuil wasn't very good at communicating + didn't finish interviews properly. He just expected people to realise they should go + look for their own site. He gave me the key to the ladies' disable toilet, where the washing machine was. I did a wash + we went for a swim in the lovely [?lake]. Our pitch is near the chemical toilet with a small gate leading out to a rocky path + down to the lake.

    The camp is packed cheek-by-jowl. People from Madrid with their microwaves + fridges in rented caravans. Cigarette smoke + [strikethrough] rock music + computer games. Very friendly + there's a baby Justin's age nearby, with his toddler brother [strikethrough: that] whom I've pushed on his bike. No shade, so Simon's put up the awning. He's had to mend the water tank strap that ripped off on its way down here,

the fridge + the clock. Lots of flies.
	The owner (55ish) is a retired pharmacist ~~who~~ from Madrid who wanted a life-change + bought 2 campsites.
Sunday
	We had a leisurely morning. Simon + Eve swam in the lake. We swam 3 times in the day. On the final swim I went to an outcrop of rocks on the far side of the lake. It was one of the best swims of my life. The campsite was unbearably hot, so we took deckchairs down to a pine grove by the lake where it was cooler.
	By late afternoon, most of the camp-site had emptied + the bar had closed so we couldn't get chips for supper. There was barely enough bread for breakfast.
	Across the lake is a bird area - 14 nesting pairs of black vultures. I may have seen one while swimming across.

We saw a white-headed eagle, we think, as we left Avila. There are lots of sparrows.
	This campsite I would give ***
Charles (too hot + crowded) *

Simon	* * *.
Although it's crowded, it's clean + the lake is marvellous. The owner has another campsite in the area.
__Monday 5th August__
We drove round the lake (+ lots of zig zags) to look at the other campsite, but it looked gloomy + there was no lake to swim in.
We reached Toledo + stayed at the Cardinal Hostal, a really lovely hotel, an old house belonging to a cardinal, wood, metal, tiles. Marvellous air con. Outside it was 40°. Sweat poured.

The river goes round W S + E & to the north, where our hotel is, it a V. steep hill.

Fortunately just near our hotel, is a huge escalator, which carries you up a large chunk of the hill. Definitely a plus! The hotel had a very good restaurant in the court yard.

__Tuesday__ We saw 2 synagogues (one of which did a bag search) + a Vizigothic Museum, El Grecos (his house shut) & the cathedral. Marvellous misericordes - a woman having a bath in a barrel with a shower curtain.
	 I really like Toledo. The streets are higgledy piggledy.
__Wednesday__
We drove on to Madrid. Susana + Eugenio were most welcoming + we stayed in their beautiful flat. Simon re parked the van, where we later got into trouble with a blue/green parking ticket. We walked to the Prado where they showed us their favourite pictures. Eugenio told us some of the history. We walked on to lunch + ate a fabulous meal. Rissoles, lamb chops, black pudding + doryfish baked in rock salt - piquant + crunchy. The puddings were good too - crepe cooked with lemon & leaves inside.

captions: The [?Family] of Carlos [?III] [?Madrid] 1800 Goya. Prado Blue boy on left in the [?] a felon (betrayed his people) Most beautifully painted [?dress] in the middle
pages.text: The [?Family] of Carlos [?III] / [?Madrid] 1800 / Goya. Prado / Blue boy on left in the [?] / a felon / (betrayed his people) / Most beautifully painted [?dress] in the middle

We caught a taxi back + had a siesta. Then Simon + Charles + Eugenio chatted. Suzana + Eve walked back to the centre - Puerta del Sol - + bought a Spanish dress for Flora with hairslide, fan + castanettes + a Spanish apron for Lizzie. Only at 10.45. did we go out for a walk + just before midnight we went to a bar, to give Charles (+ us!) a bowl of gazpacho + a sort of omlette! Suzana + Eugenio say they wouldn't normally have eaten again. It's still incredibly hot - they say hotter than usual - 40° in Toledo, but only 37° in Madrid. We have fans in the bedrooms.

__Thursday__
We left as the parking metre started at 9 am. Elevenses was an ideal spot, at the edge of a field of sunflowers under the shade of a willow tree. Charles paddled in the river but didn't fall in. It was so cool that he wore a sweater. We shopped at a super market for lunch.

captions: Susana Triana likes this artist Zurbaran still life with 4 vases 1658-64. Prado
pages.text:
Susana Triana likes this artist Zurbaran still life with 4 vases 1658-64. Prado

El Caldero where we ate

places: Embalse de la Cuerta del Pozo
pages.text:
It took ages to find the campsite, circling round the lakes. We are near the source of the R. Aouro, [strikethrough]near[/strikethrough] __El Frontal__ ****
near the Embalse de la Cuerta del Pozo. in the hills of Orbion. It promised a washing machine, but there isn't one. So I did a big handwash + then I swam (Simon + Charles paddled) in the enormous lake. There's a huge expanse of land under pine trees, but not that many campers. Graffiti + broken handles + locks in the loos. A lovely campsite.
__Friday__
	We had a restful day. Simon + Eve walked 2 miles to a bar along the lake where they rent out boats, but there were only canoes, no windsurfer.
	In the afternoon we swam. The campsite is underused. Showers I had were luke warm, the door handles broken + doors had graffiti. It's run by gov't agriculture dept. But the setting + the lake are beautiful.
__Saturday__
We left + motored around the hills to see another campsite, no lake, more crowded.

On over hilly country, skiing. We visited the Laguna Negra. Someone has written a poem about it. Really lovely. In over the pass of Puerta de Santa Inés, which is closed in winter. Down the other side we have come to a pretty place for lunch with a fast-flowing river, There are huge cliffs to the west, warm in the sunshine! Counted 14 huge birds circling. 6 or more ft. wing span. Eagles? It was amazing.

     We came out of the hills onto a plain with vines + pear trees, the main area for Rioc Rioja. Windmills + photo voltaic cells. We stopped at St. Maria Eurate (again) on the pilgrimage route. There's a stone colonnade around it.

     We reached Camping Ezcaba [illegible] north of [?A]Pamplona. It's near Suaren, where Wellington fought. It's about the best campsite we've been in. At reception 2 women chatted for ages ignoring us, very rudely. It was already

5.40. + we were in a great hurry to get the bedding into a washing machine. The last 2 campsites that advertised a washing machine didn't have one. This had 2, + they were both free + we managed to wash + dry the bedding before night. We also swam in a beautiful pool. The [illegible — obscured by tape] showers were heated by solar panels (only they didn't always work!) There were 69 steps up from the lower terrace where we were to the "[?welco-]

[Right column, partially obscured by tape:]  We had [illegible] the camp[site] restaurant. Charles + Eve had pizza. Simon had ½ rabbit. At 11.45. a group of children came out noisily to our [?terrace]. Charles wandered out to the loo in his Boxers. A very cross French camper shouted "Silence!"

__Sunday 10th August__
     We drove to look at the Bridge at Sorauren where Wellington wrote to ask for reinforcements - Paet's group. Very very hilly.
     Then on to see Roncesvalles.

We drove through Roncevalles, + the the town, because there were no parking places, but we stopped at the pass which had beautiful views. The police were checking for terrorist bombs (ETA etc).
   We arrived at a marvellous campsite, __Cave de Sauveterre de Bearn__ ***** friendly + a lovely river to ~~uto~~ wade through + swim in. Charles made the wading look so easy. We each swam + walked. It was much easier to get through the paper work in a French campsite + nice French squatter loos. We were a bit low on treats for supper + a nice English family - a man + woman from Poole - gave us a frozen Mars Bar. A woman cyclist from England has won a medal in the Beijing Olympics. There was a thunderstorm in the night. Rain in the morning. We drove up into the town to buy bread + have breakfast. Simon + Eve had walked round the town the evening before.
   There's a Romanesque church with an old Nativity capital + a capital of the gourmond + the beggar.

__Monday__ After breakfast in the town square, watching canoeists assemble, we drove on to a supermarket. The breadshop provide so much business for village centres.
   We shopped in a supermarket most of the morning + reached __Queyssac__ campsite, ***** 7k north of Bergerac, by evening, Charles' favourite campsite. It was open, good views, a lake with ducks. Simon made tea for 3 English women in a tent next door. It rained, for the first time on this trip. Really good showers.

__Tuesday__
We started breakfast out doors + moved in because of the rain. We drove off north towards David's house. We lunched by a river in the rain + then went to the Tree Museum at Chamberet. Bees were carrying an ant out of their [?observation hive]. We arrived at [?Sard's] house, [?Sas Noux], at 4, just after Thomas + [illegible] Naomi + Austin. Dylan (17) had had 6 friends to stay + had managed to tidy up the house well.

Alan + Jean + Sally + David made us very welcome. The house now has a staircase + upstairs bathroom. David + Dylan have made a patio. (But it was too cold to sit there. We chatted + chatted in the kitchen. For the first night David cooked us, steak with tomato + roquefort sauce à la Dylan.
__Wednesday__ The next night Charles put in 2 chickens while the rest of us were on a super 12 mile walk, with a beer at St. Hilaire. The house is very comfortable now.
__Thursday__
	Thomas played is viola, Dylan played his recorder. Then Jem played his guitar. Simon + Eve + Charles left. We stopped in Maymac at + shopped at a good green grocer. Charles found a field for us to have lunch. It started raining as I chopped the salad so we finished in the van.

I'm sitting in a blanket while I write this. It's so cold. We reached __St. Jean de Vaux__ where it continued to feel very cold, so we didn't swim. The grass was ominously green (lots of rain.)
	For the first time the restaurant in the village was open, so we had a meal Café du Centre. Charles had steak, Simon pintade (game bird) + I had garotted chicken. Very good food. We had bought puddings earlier in the day + went back to the van to eat them.
	We also tasted wine for William's + Bryony's wedding.
__Friday__
We picked up the 60 bottles of wine, at 9.30, + some others + set off northwards. We shopped + drove on until 6, arriving at an almost deserted campsite with one other van. It's a bit warmer.
__Ribemont__ **
